title: Module 1 quiz — Working with sales data

questions.question: What keyboard shortcut converts a selected range into an Excel Table?
questions.explanation: Ctrl + T opens the Create Table dialog. (Ctrl + L does the same on some systems but Ctrl + T is the canonical shortcut.)

questions.question: Which formula removes leading and trailing spaces from a cell?
questions.explanation: TRIM removes leading/trailing spaces and collapses internal multiple spaces to single spaces. CLEAN removes non-printing characters.

questions.question: Which structured reference correctly refers to the Revenue column of a table called Sales?
questions.explanation: Excel structured references use square brackets: TableName[ColumnName].

questions.question: Where do you find Remove Duplicates in modern Excel?
questions.explanation: Data → Remove Duplicates. You can pick which columns to compare when deciding what counts as a duplicate.

questions.question: After applying an AutoFilter, what happens if you copy the visible rows and paste them?
options: All rows (visible and hidden) are pasted
Only the visible rows are pasted
The filter is cleared and all rows are pasted
questions.explanation: Copying from a filtered table copies only the visible rows. This is one of the main reasons to use AutoFilter.

questions.question: You have NORTH and North in your Region column. Which formula standardises both to North?
questions.explanation: PROPER capitalises the first letter of each word and lowercases the rest. UPPER would give NORTH; LOWER would give north.
